rationale: The score is identical (8/10) across both evaluations, and the variant makes no reference to Kenya, location, or any geographic/demographic signal. The justification and strengths are reworded but substantively the same, both centered on GISCollective geospatial work, Dunnhumby pipeline experience, and Dlang systems expertise. The shifted concerns (routing tiles, C++ specialization vs. job-hopping) are job-relevant reframings well within run-to-run noise and do not key off the location change.
